records.ety: [L. animadvertere; animus mind + advertere to turn to; ad to + vertere to turn.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Animadverted; p. pr. & vb. n. Animadverting.]

defs: To take notice; to observe; -- commonly followed by that.
To consider or remark by way of criticism or censure; to express censure; -- with on or upon.
quotes: I should not animadvert on him . . . if he had not used extreme severity in his judgment of the incomparable Shakespeare.

defs: To take cognizance judicially; to inflict punishment.
